artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found no accessible standalone central binding Shanghai University generative-AI use guide. The best central source is an official academic committee news item stating that a temporary guide was reported and still being revised. Additional official sources document teaching exploration, an official AI learning/toolbox platform, and a technology ethics review notice that expressly covers AI and big-data teaching/research activities involving people or animals.

artifacts.title: Shanghai University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Official Shanghai University discovery did not locate an accessible standalone central generative-AI use guide. The strongest central evidence is an official academic committee report stating that a temporary guide was presented and still being revised, with aims around responsible use, academic integrity, data security, and AI-enabled education reform. Additional official sources show teaching exploration, official AI learning/toolbox platform listings, and technology-ethics review requirements for AI and big-data related teaching/research activities involving people, animals, or technology-ethics matters.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims do not assert that Shanghai University has published a central binding generative-AI policy because the crawl did not locate the standalone guide text.
The AI toolbox listing should not be read as approval to use listed tools for assessed work.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
